channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: Temu fined more than $230 million by EU over illegal product sales
item.description: Temu has been fined €200 million (about $232 million) by the European Commission after it found that consumers are "very likely to encounter illegal items" on the popular Chinese e-commerce...

item.title: 427 Remote Companies Using TypeScript in 2026
item.description: Every frontend developer puts TypeScript on their resume now. So does every fullstack. So does every backend engineer on a JS team. So do most React Native people, most Next.js people, and a large...

item.title: MCP CI gates need receipts: tools/list is not enough
item.description: MCP servers are starting to look like normal infrastructure. That means they need boring infrastructure checks. The mistake I kept seeing is this: "The server starts, and tools/list returns a...

item.title: I Generated a Tableau Dashboard Using Gemma 4 — Locally, No API Key, No Cloud
item.description: Why I Wanted to Try This With Gemma 4 I’ve been building Twilize — a tool that generates Tableau workbooks from natural language — for a while now. Most of my own testing uses the Anthropic API...

item.title: The Hidden Way Electronics Can Start a Fire — Even Without an Open Flame
item.description: Most people assume that electronics catch fire because of an external flame or a dramatic short circuit. In reality, quite a few fires start from something much less obvious: a small connection or...

item.title: I Built a Beginner-Friendly NGINX Automation CLI for Linux Servers
item.description: I’ve configured NGINX manually more times than I can count. Every server setup usually turns into the same repetitive process: install nginx configure virtual hosts set up reverse proxies issue...

item.title: Vibe Thinking - The PM Who Writes Requirements That an AI Can Actually Use
item.description: The dev team is moving fast. Requirements come in, developers build quickly, and then the demo happens. The outcome isn't what was wanted. The brief was technically correct but the result was...

item.title: A Refreshing Perspective on AI and Truth
item.description: Everyone has a favorite movie. Some of us ask why. A kid might say Spiderverse. A cinephile might insist on Lawrence of Arabia. A film historian might point further back — to a Buster Keaton...

item.title: Kubelet Metrics: How cAdvisor and CRI Collect Kubernetes Stats
item.description: This article was originally published on LearnKube TL;DR: This article dissects the Kubernetes metrics pipeline through kubelet, cAdvisor, and CRI to show where your metrics actually come from and...

item.title: How to Optimize MongoDB on Bare Metal Servers: SRE Playbook
item.description: The explosion of artificial intelligence retrieval applications has transformed the way enterprises deploy document databases. However, transitioning from managed cloud platforms to massive bare...

item.title: Why I Built Bamise Instead of Using Laravel
item.description: Laravel is one of the best things that ever happened to PHP. It transformed modern PHP development, introduced elegant developer experience patterns, improved the ecosystem massively, and made web...

item.title: Gemini for Google Home can now use your cameras to trigger automations
item.description: Google Home is rolling out a new Gemini-powered automation feature that can trigger smart home routines based on what your security cameras can see. This is one of several updates announced...

item.title: New Moms Are Returning to Coding Jobs Radically Reshaped by AI
item.description: New mothers working in software development are staring down an AI-pilled workplace they barely recognize.

item.title: Amazon Thinks the Future of Data Centers Depends on a Technical Problem It Just Solved
item.description: The tech giant says a breakthrough in data-center networking has dramatically accelerated the flow of information through its massive cloud infrastructure.

item.title: Climate tech companies are going public. What’s next?
item.description: This year, there’s been a wave of notable energy companies going public via IPO in the US. The solar and battery company Solv Energy went public in February, to the tune of $6 billion. X-energy,...

item.title: The AI Hype Index: AI gets booed in graduation season
item.description: It is one thing to say AI will change the world. It is another to expect the class of 2026 to applaud it. In fact, when former Google CEO Eric Schmidt told University of Arizona graduates that...

item.title: YouTube will let you ask AI to make a custom video feed
item.description: YouTube is launching a new AI feature that creates a personalized video feed based on descriptions of what you want to watch. In its announcement, YouTube says custom content feeds can be built...

item.title: JINX-0164 Targets Cryptocurrency Firms with Fake Recruiter Lures and macOS Malware
item.description: A new campaign orchestrated by a previously undocumented threat actor has targeted cryptocurrency organizations with an aim to facilitate digital asset theft using recruitment-themed social...

item.title: Vertu wants CEOs to run companies from an AI foldable starting at $6,880
item.description: Built on top of the open-source Hermes project, Vertu's new foldable combines AI-agent workflows, enterprise integrations, and ultra-premium luxury finishes.

item.title: The golden age of handheld gaming is already over
item.description: For a few glorious years, a $399 portable gadget could run almost anything you'd want to play. In 2022, the Steam Deck finally made PC gaming portable and affordable. I played through the vast...

item.title: Why Google’s AI can’t spell Google (or anything else)
item.description: Google is embarrassing itself, again.

item.title: Illinois Lawmakers Just Passed America’s Strongest AI Safety Bill
item.description: The bill requires companies like OpenAI, Anthropic, and Google to have third parties confirm they’re following safety standards. Illinois governor JB Pritzker says he’ll sign it.

item.title: A Google employee allegedly used inside information to win $1.2 million on Polymarket
item.description: Federal prosecutors charged a Google employee with fraud after he allegedly made $1.2 million on Polymarket bets related to Search-related trends in 2025, as reported earlier by ABC News. In their...

item.title: Apple’s newest iPad Air is up to $100 off for the first time
item.description: If you want an iPad that’s more powerful than the entry-level model but less expensive than the iPad Pro, the latest iPad Air is the one to buy, and it’s down to one its best prices to date. The...

item.title: Websites have a new way to spy on visitors: analyzing their SSD activity
item.description: Telltale SSD activity can be measured in the browser using simple JavaScript.

item.title: All the news about Ferrari’s polarizing Luce EV
item.description: Ferrari fans don’t like the design of the new Luce EV, an electric four-door sedan that just doesn’t look like the Ferraris of old. It was designed with help from Jony Ive’s LoveFrom, but what...

item.title: In more good news for Amazon, Snowflake signs $6B deal with AWS for AI CPU chips
item.description: Snowflake has signed a new, enormous five-year deal with Amazon to secure chips for AI usage. Nvidia is once again being put on notice.

item.title: The new Halide camera app launches with film looks and an upgraded photo editor
item.description: After first being announced in December 2024, Lux Optics has finally released the latest version of its Halide camera app for the iPhone and iPad. The Halide Mark III app's most compelling feature...

item.title: Facebook launches a ‘Plus’ subscription that gives you extra features
item.description: After announcing tests of premium subscriptions for Facebook, Instagram, WhatsApp earlier this year, TechCrunch and Bloomberg report that Meta is launching a global rollout over the next few weeks...

item.title: Payroll startup Remote says it grew revenue 50% per employee without adding headcount
item.description: Payroll service provider Remote recently surpassed $300 million in annual recurring revenue (ARR) and became cash-flow positive, thanks to a 50% increase in revenue per employee resulting from AI...
